scoreConversionMap.topic: Encoder block from memory → implementation points
VAE from memory + ELBO derivation → theory + implementation
GAN objective, mode collapse, WGAN → short-answer
Full-task pipeline in 75 min on GPU → pipeline points
Kernel proof + PAC bound derivation → proof points
SVM dual + KKT (why α_i > 0 for support vectors) → theory
Softmax + CE gradient, backprop through affine layer
Diffusion forward/reverse + UNet skeleton → conceptual + code
CLIP / InfoNCE + SD text conditioning → differentiation points
Reasoning sections: justify loss, init, optimizer choice
Papers With Code browsing, ecosystem knowledge

resourceTimeCap.rule: Primary paper or note — read the section needed, then close it
One implementation reference — study the key lines, then close it
Rebuild from memory — this is the minimum, not the maximum
Third resource unlocked only if mistake log proves first two failed

redFlagList: Reading instead of rebuilding. If you spent more time reading than producing from memory today, that session was low-ROI.
Polishing GAN or UNet code. Aim for literacy, not craft. A working skeleton earns full credit.
Chasing architecture breadth. Every hour on ViT / LoRA / Score SDEs is an hour not spent on a 3rd attention-from-memory repetition.
Under-practicing reasoning sections. The 2026 format awards Markdown reasoning. If you have not written 3+ reasoning cells this week, you are behind.
Not timing derivations. "I know this" ≠ "I can produce this cold in 8 minutes." Timed production is the only test that matters.
Conflating understanding with production. You can explain attention perfectly and still fail to implement it under pressure. Rebuild counts; recall alone does not.

syllabusBlindSpots.title: Kernel Validity: Mercer Closure Proofs
syllabusBlindSpots.formula: Mercer's Condition: K is valid iff for all z: z^T K z >= 0 (K is PSD)
keyPoints: Sum: K1 and K2 valid => K = K1 + K2 is valid
Product: K1 and K2 valid => K = K1 * K2 is valid
Exponential: K valid => K' = exp(K(x,x')) is valid
RBF = exp(-||x-x'||^2 / 2sigma^2) is valid and why

syllabusBlindSpots.title: PAC Learning & Generalization Bounds
keyPoints: Derivation chain: Hoeffding -> Union Bound -> PAC Bound
Practice: given n=1000, |H|=100, what confidence on error <= 0.1?
Understand what n, |H|, and eps each control

syllabusBlindSpots.title: CLIP / InfoNCE Contrastive Loss
syllabusBlindSpots.formula: InfoNCE: L = -(1/N) sum log [exp(sim(zi,ti)/tau) / sum_j exp(sim(zi,tj)/tau)]
keyPoints: CLIP: image encoder (ViT) + text encoder (Transformer) -> shared latent space
InfoNCE: maximize similarity of N correct pairs, minimize N^2-N incorrect
Temperature tau controls how peaked the distribution is
Cross-entropy over similarity scores, not reconstruction loss

syllabusBlindSpots.title: Stable Diffusion: VAE Decoder + Text Conditioning
syllabusBlindSpots.formula: CLIP text encoder -> UNet (cross-attention on text embeddings) -> VAE decoder -> pixel output
keyPoints: VAE Decoder: diffusion in latent space, VAE decoder maps denoised latent back to pixels
Text conditioning: CLIP embeddings injected via cross-attention in UNet residual blocks
Noisy latent is Q; text embeddings are K and V

syllabusBlindSpots.title: Reasoning Section Drill (2026 Format Change)
syllabusBlindSpots.formula: 10-Minute Reasoning Section Protocol after every implementation
keyPoints: Why this loss function? (BCE vs MSE for VAE)
Why this weight initialization? (Xavier vs Kaiming)
What would break if you changed X?

scorePillars.name: Transformer Mastery
items: Derive scaled dot-product attention cleanly
Explain multi-head attention, positional encoding, residuals, layer norm
Implement a transformer encoder block from memory
Understand masking and decoder structure conceptually
Know Flash Attention conceptually: IO-aware recomputation

scorePillars.name: Generative AI Mastery
items: Autoencoder vs VAE vs GAN vs diffusion: purpose and differences
Derive ELBO at competition level
Implement a minimal VAE from memory
Explain GAN objective and mode collapse
Explain DDPM/stable diffusion conceptually; implement UNet
CLIP: InfoNCE loss, dual-encoder architecture, temperature tau
SD pipeline: CLIP encoder -> UNet (cross-attention) -> VAE decoder

scorePillars.name: Math / Derivation Fluency
items: Softmax + cross-entropy gradient
Backprop through linear layers, activation, normalization
Vector projections, norms, orthogonality, matrix shape discipline
SVM dual and kernel validity at proof level
Mercer closure proofs: sum, product, exp of valid kernels
PAC learning bound: P(R(f_n) >= eps) <= |H| * e^(-n*eps)
Hoeffding / concentration style applications

scorePillars.name: Competition Execution
items: Solve under time pressure
Write clear markdown/LaTeX explanations
Reasoning sections: explain why behind every architecture and loss choice
Complete full-task pipelines on GPU
Avoid shape mistakes, algebra slips, vague reasoning

dailyNonNegotiables.task: PyTorch / NumPy / linear algebra flash review (no docs)
One handwritten derivation or proof fragment
Log mistakes with a fix action in the taxonomy (category + what you will do tonight to fix it)

days.title: Scaled dot-product attention from first principles
tasks: Read "Attention Is All You Need" section 3 (architecture only)
Derive softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k))V on paper - track all shapes, understand why sqrt(d_k) scaling prevents softmax saturation
Implement single-head attention in NumPy from scratch, timed
Verify: multiply random Q, K, V matrices, check shapes manually

resources.name: The Illustrated Transformer
resources.note: use when paper feels too abstract

days.title: Multi-head attention + positional encoding
tasks: Implement multi-head attention: split -> parallel heads -> concat -> linear projection
Write shape table from memory: (batch, heads, seq_len, d_k)
Derive sinusoidal positional encoding formula; implement from scratch
Review residual connection + layer norm purpose
All in PyTorch on GPU

days.title: Full encoder block in PyTorch
tasks: Implement from memory: multi-head attention -> residual + LayerNorm -> FFN -> residual + LayerNorm
Understand pre-norm vs post-norm - know which the original paper uses and why pre-norm is now preferred
Verify forward pass shape correctness on GPU

days.title: Masking + decoder concepts
tasks: Implement causal mask (upper triangular) for autoregressive decoding
Understand encoder self-attention vs masked self-attention vs cross-attention
Understand why cross-attention uses encoder output for K, V
Small coding drill for mask creation only

days.title: Attention gradients on paper
tasks: Derive dL/dQ, dL/dK, dL/dV for scaled dot-product attention by hand
Focus on correctness, not elegance
Write up in LaTeX in Colab markdown cell, timed (practice competition format)

days.title: Linear algebra repair day
tasks: Norms, projections, orthogonality, unit vectors
Matrix multiplication shape checks
Fix residual mistakes from older work
Connect back to attention: attention weights are projections in embedding space

days.title: Timed transformer drill [REP 1 — attention from memory]
tasks: 45-min no-doc implementation: attention + MHA + residual + LN + FFN
30-min written explanation of each component
If you can't finish: identify exactly what tripped you up and redo

days.title: Autoencoder + VAE
tasks: Build vanilla autoencoder on MNIST: encoder -> bottleneck -> decoder (MSE or BCE)
Extend to VAE: encoder outputs mu and log-variance
Understand reparameterization trick: z = mu + sigma * epsilon (gradient can't flow through sampling)
All on GPU

resources.name: Lilian Weng: From Autoencoder to Beta-VAE

days.title: ELBO derivation + VAE from memory + CLIP intro
tasks: Morning: Derive log p(x) >= E_q[log p(x|z)] - KL(q(z|x) || p(z)) on paper
Understand each term: reconstruction loss + KL regularization
Write one full clean derivation in markdown/LaTeX
Afternoon: 30-45 min timed VAE from memory
CLIP (30 min): Read CLIP paper Section 2 — dual-encoder, InfoNCE loss, temperature tau

resources.name: Lilian Weng: From Autoencoder to Beta-VAE

days.title: GAN fundamentals
tasks: Implement simple GAN on MNIST: generator + discriminator
Minimax objective: min_G max_D E[log D(x)] + E[log(1 - D(G(z)))]
Understand mode collapse - why it happens
Know conceptually: Wasserstein distance and WGAN stability

resources.name: Lilian Weng: From GAN to WGAN

days.title: GAN implementation practice
tasks: Minimal MNIST GAN or pseudo-code level
Explain training alternation (train D, freeze G; then train G, freeze D)
Experience instability firsthand

days.title: Diffusion models - conceptual + SD architecture
tasks: Forward noising process: x_t = sqrt(alpha_bar_t) * x_0 + sqrt(1-alpha_bar_t) * epsilon
Reverse denoising: learn to predict noise epsilon given x_t and t
Noise prediction loss: ||epsilon - epsilon_theta(x_t, t)||^2
UNet role as the denoising network
Full SD pipeline: CLIP text encoder -> UNet (cross-attention) -> VAE decoder -> pixels
Text conditioning: noisy latent is Q, CLIP text embeddings are K and V

resources.name: Lilian Weng: What are Diffusion Models?

days.title: UNet skeleton + skip-connection shape verification
tasks: Implement UNet skeleton only: encoder (conv + downsample) -> bottleneck -> decoder (upsample + skip concat)
Goal: verify skip-connection shapes match and forward pass runs on GPU — not a polished implementation
Stop when it works at a basic level. Log any shape errors immediately.
UNet is on the syllabus as backbone of diffusion models — literacy is enough, craft is not

days.title: Timed generative review
tasks: 25-min VAE rebuild from memory (no docs)
20-min written compare/contrast: AE vs VAE, VAE vs GAN, GAN vs diffusion
15-min GAN objective + instability explanation in LaTeX

resources.name: Lilian Weng: From Autoencoder to Beta-VAE
Lilian Weng: From GAN to WGAN

days.title: SVM fundamentals + implementation
tasks: Max-margin classifier: find hyperplane that maximizes distance to nearest points
Hinge loss: max(0, 1 - y * f(x))
Support vectors and soft-margin SVM with C parameter
Implement soft-margin SVM from scratch in NumPy/PyTorch

resources.name: CS229 Full Lecture Notes (2022)
resources.note: single PDF for all classical ML

days.title: SVM dual + Lagrangian + KKT depth
tasks: Derive the dual formulation via Lagrange multipliers
Understand why the dual is useful: kernel trick only works in dual form
KKT depth: Know why alpha_i > 0 only for support vectors (complementary slackness)
Focus on deriving structure, not memorizing symbols

days.title: Kernel trick + kernel validity proofs (Mercer's condition)
tasks: Kernel trick: K(x, y) = phi(x) . phi(y) without computing phi
Common kernels: linear, polynomial, RBF
Prove kernel validity: Gram matrix is PSD (z^T K z >= 0 for all z)
Three closure proofs from memory: sum, product, exp of valid kernels
Know RBF = exp(-||x-x'||^2 / 2sigma^2) is valid and why

days.title: Backprop and matrix calculus
tasks: Derive gradient of softmax + cross-entropy combined
MSE vectorized gradient
Basic chain rule through affine layer
Optional: batch norm gradient if core gradients are solid

days.title: Probability / concentration inequalities + PAC bounds
tasks: Hoeffding's inequality: statement, proof sketch, application — cold-recallable
Chebyshev's inequality quick review
PAC learning: Derive P(R(f_n) >= eps) <= |H| * e^(-n*eps)
Derivation chain: Hoeffding -> Union Bound -> PAC Bound
Practice: given n=1000, |H|=100, what confidence on error <= 0.1?

days.title: Timed proof day + Mercer kernel closure [REP 1 — kernel/PAC proofs]
tasks: 20 min: kernel validity proofs from memory — PSD definition + all three closure cases
20 min: ELBO derivation sketch
20 min: attention gradient explanation (dL/dQ, dL/dK, dL/dV)
20 min: backprop gradient sketch
Honest assessment: which proofs are locked in, which need another pass

days.title: Convex optimization + review
tasks: Convex sets and functions: definition, examples, verification
Gradient descent convergence: why convexity guarantees global minimum
KKT conditions worked examples - connect to SVM dual
Light review of Phase 1-3 weak spots from mistake log

days.title: Past-problem diagnostic
tasks: Take one past Round 2 style task (forum.beaver-edge.ai, IOAI 2024 Scientific Round)
Simulate time pressure exactly
Diagnose failure modes - map each to mistake taxonomy
This sets the agenda for the rest of spring break

days.title: Full-task drill #1 - Tabular classification pipeline
tasks: Morning: Full tabular pipeline in 90 min, no docs
pd.read_csv -> explore -> handle missing -> encode -> normalize -> train/val -> PyTorch -> GPU -> evaluate -> save -> predict
Afternoon: Redo on different dataset, targeting 75 min
Reasoning section (10 min): Why this loss function, why this optimizer, what breaks if you change CE to MSE

days.title: Math repair day + attention repetition [REP 2 — attention + kernel/PAC from memory]
tasks: Use mistake log only for repairs. No new topics.
Morning: PAC bound from memory, Chebyshev applications, Mercer closure proofs from memory (second repetition)
Afternoon: Attention from memory, target 35 min, no docs. Then matrix calculus redo.

days.title: Full-task drill #2 - Image classification pipeline
tasks: Morning: CIFAR-10 full pipeline in 90 min, no docs
Afternoon: Review all proofs from memory in LaTeX — attention grads, ELBO, Mercer+closure, SVM dual+KKT, Hoeffding->PAC
Reasoning section (10 min): Xavier vs Kaiming init, pre-norm vs post-norm LayerNorm

days.title: Mixed simulation + kernel/PAC repetition [REP 2 — kernel/PAC + REP 3 — attention]
tasks: 45 min: Coding - transformer encoder OR VAE from memory (weaker one)
30 min: Theory writeup - derivation + explanation
30 min: Kernel closure + PAC bound from scratch, timed — second repetition block
Reasoning section (10 min): Adam vs SGD for transformers, temperature scaling in attention
15 min: Light NLP review (BERT, GPT objectives, fine-tuning — conceptual only)

days.title: Weak spot blitz + attention repetition [REP 3 — attention from memory, tight time]
tasks: Morning: Timed implementations (transformer encoder 40min — third repetition no notes, VAE 25min, GAN 25min)
Afternoon: Practice reasoning explanations in Colab Markdown (2-3 practice explanations)
Only redo the two weakest areas from mistake log

days.title: Full competition simulation - dress rehearsal
tasks: 3-hour timed session simulating Round 2 exactly
Problem 1: Theory derivation in LaTeX (45 min)
Problem 2: From-scratch model implementation (60 min)
Problem 3: Full-task pipeline on GPU (75 min)
After: Honest debrief. Rest the brain. Pack for MIT. Sleep by 10pm. The work is done.

nightBefore: No new content. Review mistake log once.
Prepare: laptop, charger, Colab GPU confirmed.
Have a .py backup of encoder block, VAE skeleton, and training loop stub ready to paste if session drops.
competitionDay.morningWarmup: 15-min attention derivation on paper. Do not review anything new. Arrive early and confirm GPU environment before the clock starts.

tacticalTimeSplit.instruction: Read the entire problem set before writing a single line. Identify where the points are concentrated.
Start with what you know best — not Problem #1. Bank partial credit early.
Write assumptions, define variables, show all derivation steps even if incomplete. Partial credit is real.
Write a shape table before every tensor operation. If code fails after 10 min of debugging, switch to explanation + pseudocode immediately — explanation credit exists.
Write your assumptions and what you would do next. Graders award method points. A blank page earns nothing.
Reserve 10-15 min per problem for clean Markdown reasoning. This is where differentiation points live.
Know how to reconnect and reload in under 60 seconds if session drops. Test this during the Apr 3 simulation.

competitionDay.motto: Score is the sum of mechanics repeated until automatic. There is nothing to discover on competition day. Execute what is already built.

mustHave.label: Derive scaled dot-product attention from scratch
Explain multi-head attention, positional encoding, residuals, layer norm
Implement transformer encoder block from memory (target: 45 min)
Explain GAN objective + training instability + mode collapse
Explain diffusion forward/reverse process and noise prediction loss
Prove basic kernel validity: PSD definition + sum/product/exp closure proofs
Explain SVM dual at a clean high level + why alpha_i > 0 only for support vectors
Derive PAC bound from Hoeffding + union bound
Complete a full-task pipeline in 75 min on GPU
Write readable markdown/LaTeX under time pressure
Write reasoning sections: explain why behind loss, init, optimizer choices
Comfortable with GPU workflow: .to(device), batch sizing, OOM

niceToHave.label: CLIP: InfoNCE loss, dual-encoder architecture, temperature tau
Stable Diffusion: VAE decoder role + text conditioning via cross-attention in UNet
LoRA: rank decomposition delta_W = BA, know when and why
Implement simple GAN from memory (target: 30 min)

mistakeTaxonomy.fix: Timed drill with 10% less time than last attempt

lowROIExtras.guidance: Read the abstract only. Know: attention is memory-bandwidth bound, not compute-bound. Close it.
Interesting, not testable at this level. Skip.

lowROIExtras.status: Only if diffusion goes beyond DDPM

lowROIExtras.guidance: One sentence: ΔW = BA where r << d. That's it.
Read Fig 1 and first paragraph. Stop.

lowROIExtras.status: Only if UNet skeleton is solid
lowROIExtras.guidance: Full repo study is a time sink.
Conceptual only during Apr 1 NLP review.

priorityOrder: VAE / GAN / diffusion conceptual mastery + UNet (Pillar B)
Backprop + matrix calculus + linear algebra cleanup (Pillar C)
SVM dual + kernel proofs + Mercer closure (Pillar C)
Full-task pipeline speed on GPU (Pillar D)
CLIP + SD conditioning (nice-to-have that could become must-have)
